issuesFound.title: Page title is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. Users relying on screen readers or browser tabs cannot identify the page content or distinguish it from other tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element for the hero dashboard screenshot lacks an alt attribute. Screen reader users will not hear a description of this informative image.
issuesFound.fix: Line 128:

issuesFound.title: Link text is non-descriptive
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Learn more" does not describe the destination without surrounding context. Screen reader users navigating by links cannot distinguish this from other "Learn more" links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 159: Learn more about the accessibility scanner

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button contains only an icon with aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users hear nothing when focusing this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 28: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Toggle main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users do not know what data to enter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 770: <input type="email" id="newsletter-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com" aria-label="Email address for newsletter">

issuesFound.title: Input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The first name input collects personal data but lacks an autocomplete attribute. Users with cognitive disabilities or motor impairments may not benefit from browser autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 806: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element "Feature" lacks a scope attribute. In a table with row headers, column headers need scope="col" to be properly associated with data cells.
issuesFound.fix: Line 580: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on the page. This primary navigation lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 26: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Section landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <section> elements on the page. This hero section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish it from other sections.
issuesFound.fix: Line 103: <section class="hero" aria-label="Hero introduction">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The section references aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document. The landmark has no valid accessible name.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 169:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 (Line 238) to h5 (Line 248) without intermediate h3 or h4 levels. This disrupts the logical outline for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 248:

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to identify the focused element. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline without adding a box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating buttons will not see which one is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus state removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the logo link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 429: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus without a replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 458: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus within the submenu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 496: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input :focus-visible rule removes the outline and does not re-apply the box-shadow defined in the :focus rule. Keyboard users lose the focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 528: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 543: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 558: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the active filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 873: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 966: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on this region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1038: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused question.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1139: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on :focus-visible without re-applying the box-shadow from the :focus rule. Keyboard users lose the focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1174: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1182: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1192: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1202: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1212: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has a fixed height and width of 28px with 0 padding. This is below the recommended 44px touch target size, making it difficult for touch users to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 20px. Even with padding, the total height may not reach 44px, and 20px is below the 24px absolute minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 693: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 709: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has a fixed width and height of 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1181: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually. This exposes screen-reader-only content to sighted users, cluttering the interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transition: transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 439 is empty and does not disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 439: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transition: transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rule at Line 622 is empty and does not disable the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rule that disables this transition for buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 653: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing to -0.05em. Negative letter-spacing reduces the space between characters, impairing readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 234: body { letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .newsletter-section
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section sets white text (#ffffff) on a blue/purple gradient background (#5588bb to #bb88ee). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.38:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: .newsletter-section { color: #000000; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 273: a { text-decoration: underline; }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer by adding/removing CSS classes, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1408: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1426: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` functions show/hide the dropdown panel but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1622: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1636: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function adds the open class and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). The `close()` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2333: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute on the field. The field may remain marked as invalid in the accessibility tree even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1206: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function locates the trigger button but does not initialize it with `aria-expanded="false"`. Assistive technologies need this initial state to understand the control is a toggle that starts closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1467: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.register()` function sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` on the trigger but omits the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute. This leaves the initial state undefined for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1727: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge()` function updates the `aria-label` of the badge element but does not update the text content of an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users are not notified when the product count changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3029: const liveRegion = qs('[aria-live="polite"]'); if (liveRegion) liveRegion.textContent = `${count} products found`;

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._updateSavingsMessage()` function updates the text content of `_savingsMsg` but does not ensure the element has `aria-live` or announce the change via the shared `liveRegion`. Users switching billing periods miss the savings announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2799: liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent);

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters()` function updates the grid visibility and emits a custom event but does not announce the filter change (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users are unaware the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod()` function updates prices and emits a custom event but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users are not informed of the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2753: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. The button contains only an emoji icon, so screen reader users cannot identify its purpose (switching themes).
issuesFound.fix: Line 629: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.title: Disclosure toggle trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav submenu trigger button controls a list (via aria-controls) but lacks aria-expanded. Users cannot tell if the submenu is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 866: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: Filter toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The FilterTabs buttons track active state via activeFilter but do not use aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which category filter is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1153: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}>

issuesFound.title: Dialog panel missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav panel has role="dialog" but is missing aria-modal="true". Assistive technology may not correctly constrain focus or indicate that interaction is limited to this dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 842: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not pass aria-busy={isLoading} to the underlying button element. Screen reader users are not notified that the button is in a busy/loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 466: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection uses a <section> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this section from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Section landmark missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby. The landmark lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 967: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple navs on the page, this landmark is indistinguishable from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Required field marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk (*) indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "asterisk" unnecessarily if the field is already marked required programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: *

issuesFound.title: Star rating decorative icons not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component labels the container, but the individual star spans (★/☆) are not hidden. This causes redundancy where screen readers may read the label plus each star character.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: <span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'}>

issuesFound.title: Duplicate content not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The IntegrationsBar renders a cloned list of logos for visual looping effects without aria-hidden. Screen reader users will encounter the same list of integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1053: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown chevron span contains text characters (▲/▼) that are decorative within the button context. They should be hidden to prevent cluttering the accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 812: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: Statistic value missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem renders the formatted value (e.g., "40,000+") which may be read awkwardly by screen readers. The stat.valueLabel (e.g., "40,000 plus") provided in props is ignored.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 953:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Card CTA missing descriptive aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard CTA link text does not explicitly include the plan name (e.g., "Get started for free" vs "Starter"). In a grid of similar cards, unique aria-labels including the item name are required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1437: <a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} for ${plan.name} plan`} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md',].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". This prevents assistive technology from correctly identifying the dialog as modal.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2079: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line 2083: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

fpTitles: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
